programme.desc: Daibhidh Walker reads Hamish agus an Trèana, written and illustrated by Daisy Hirst, and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald

programme.desc: Ronia is chased when she’s out, and she comes across something that she is not expecting

programme.desc: Tha tòrr plòigh a’ tachairt ‘s iad a’ putadh na cloiche an-diugh. There’s lots of fun happening as they shot-put today.

programme.title: Bruadar a' Bhàis (A Dream of Death)
programme.desc: When a young man was convicted of murder in Inverness in 1830, it was the only time in Scots legal history that testimony from second sight was admitted in court. This dramatised documentary revisits the crime and subsequent trial

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads head out of the kitchen to Harris to visit Roddy's father for home-grown potatoes, and his cousin for cockles to make chowder. Uisdean sources seafood for a Jambalaya

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: In Ethopia, as children wake up to the first rays of sunshine the temperature is already over 30 degrees Celsius. They live in the Danakil desert in the north-east of the country, near an active volcano, in a region that is the world's hottest on average. Getting to school is quite an ordeal, especially if they encounter sandstorms

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum Maclean heads to Applecross in Wester Ross, travelling from the edge of the sea to the top of hills to catch that special photo

programme.title: Mach à Seo!
programme.desc: Ramsay MacMahon ditches the car and makes his way on foot, following the Fife Coastal Path, between the pretty seaside villages of Earlsferry and Pittenweem

programme.title: Creag nam Buthaidean (Puffin Rock)
programme.desc: Chasing his wooly ball, Baba finds himself in a seagull's nest on the cliff edge. However, a storm is raging and heading for the rock, putting the little pufflings in danger

programme.desc: Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A focus on the early days of famous sports topics

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the language and landscape of the Cuillin, a range of rocky mountains on the Isle of Skye. Beginning his journey at Bruach na Frithe with local mountain rescuer Ewen MacKinnon, he then meets Allan Turner on a walk from Coruisk to Glen Sligachan, who explains the history of the area

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Sweeny questions his future as a full time crofter. As he gets organised for an essential day trip to Uist, he shares his thoughts on how he feels about making the journey

programme.title: Is Mise an Teanga
programme.desc: Documentary about the Gaelic language, with contributions by contemporary poets from the Outer Hebrides, Dublin, Connemara, West Belfast and Glasgow

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Featuring award-winning Scots singer Iona Fyfe, accompanied by multi-instrumentalist Graeme Rorie and guitarist Jack McRobbie

programme.title: Eilbheas
programme.desc: Drama set on the island of Lewis on the day of Elvis Presley's death in 1977. A man's life is transformed by an unexpected encounter with the undisputed king of rock 'n' roll. Starring Greg Hemphill and Sean MacLeod

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic
programme.desc: Duncan Chisholm and the Scottish Ensemble perform at the Kelvingrove Museum and Art Gallery as a part of Celtic Connections

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean makes his way to Glen Brittle on the Isle of Skye to discover a waterfall called Eas Mòr, which is located at the foot of the Cuillin and offers a wonderful landscape

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The autumn chill is turning the forest gold when King Sigurd takes Gudrun to gather nuts for a feast. However, after Gudrun falls asleep beside a tree she awakes to find her basket empty. She's not the only one gathering nuts for the winter

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais
programme.desc: When Coinneach the cockerel decides that he is the best person to be the farm leader, Mona challenges him and, as a result of this, they put it to a vote

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: A selection of seafaring songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Moonfish tells us that they are the only fish who have a warm heart and that their bodies are warmer than the waters around them when they swim to the depths of the ocean

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo?discovers little seeds in an apple. From each seed a plant can grow, and each plant can turn into a tree

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.desc: Pàdraig is asked to make an unusual delivery to the school as the pupils prepare for their play - a special yeti suit

programme.title: Na Moomins/Moomin Valley
programme.desc: Instead of continuing with his hibernation, Mùmantrobha is drawn into taking part in a winter sports competition with athlete Monseigneur Brisg

programme.title: A' Chuil
programme.desc: Choirstaidh is back with another programme from her studio A' Chùil. She makes a sign, and other things, out of string to decorate her studio

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach
programme.desc: Fearchar tries out the gymnastics rings

programme.title: Facal Gaidhlig CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: Calum Macdonald introduces a selection of his favourite Gaelic words

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news and information from BBC ALBA

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee introduce highlights of the second day of the International Sheepdog Trials in Wales

programme.title: Is Mise an Teanga
programme.desc: Documentary about the Gaelic language, with contributions by contemporary poets from the Outer Hebrides, Dublin, Connemara, West Belfast and Glasgow

programme.title: Binneas
programme.desc: Special performances from the acclaimed band Old Blind Dogs, recorded for the Trad Awards 2020

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the language and landscape of the Cuillin, a range of rocky mountains on the Isle of Skye. Beginning his journey at Bruach na Frithe with local mountain rescuer Ewen MacKinnon, he then meets Allan Turner on a walk from Coruisk to Glen Sligachan, who explains the history of the area

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Following a group of individuals from all over the country, bringing a unique insight through detailed personal accounts of the beginning of 2021, when the government announced that Scotland would go back into lockdown

programme.title: Cuirm @ Celtic
programme.desc: A compilation programme from Celtic Connections 2021

programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: In Ethopia, as children wake up to the first rays of sunshine the temperature is already over 30 degrees Celsius. They live in the Danakil desert in the north-east of the country, near an active volcano, in a region that is the world's hottest on average. Getting to school is quite an ordeal, especially if they encounter sandstorms

programme.title: Dealbh is Slighe
programme.desc: Calum travels across Loch Awe to Kilchurn Castle, looking for the perfect light to take a special photo

programme.title: Ceòl Dùthchasach/Ken Burns' Country Music
programme.desc: The history of the art form and how it evolved between 1953 and 1963, when the confluence of blues and hillbilly music gave birth to rockabilly, the precursor of rock 'n' roll

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean swims at the Falls of Lora, where River Etive meets The Firth of Lorne. He often looks for a challenge and tries to find locations that other swimmers do not frequent

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson helps members of the public in Solihull sort the treasures from their trash. Simon Schneider gets into a movie poster shoot-out, while Fay Rutter eyes up a gent's Rolex. Henry Nicholls spends cash to not own some saucy postcards, while the auction leads to an emotional conclusion

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, which sees a family of five from Liverpool competing to answer questions on subjects including Monty Python, English monarch and classic comics. They then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith faces her fate at the hospital. Andrea and Charles cannot resist each other. Leanna is upset with her father

programme.title: Eat, Shop, Save
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and her team of experts are in North Wales to help a family to combat the carb-heavy convenience food, and rein in their supermarket spending

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith gives the wrong idea. Vic and David bond over their babies. Ben takes a mysterious and unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Martin Clunes: My Travels and Other Animals
programme.desc: The actor learns how to look after vulnerable creatures as he travels the world. Martin heads to the Ningaloo reef in Australia and swims with the world's biggest fish, the whale shark, before taking part in the oldest Royal tradition - swan upping - on the River Thames

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part two of two. Three-year-olds Callie and Carter are approaching their fourth birthday, and parents Nick and Chelsea are under pressure to make a decision about whether to keep them joined together or put them through surgery to separate them. They meet other conjoined twins to help them decide

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Switzerland v Spain (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the first quarter-final at Saint Petersburg Stadium, as both sides face each other after progressing in two thrilling round-of-16 clashes that both needed extra time. Spain threw away a 3-1 lead with just five minutes to go in their previous fixture against Croatia, before finally winning the game 5-3 thanks to two quick goals from Alvaro Morata and Mike Oyarzabal. The Swiss beat favourites France 5-4 on penalties after also coming back from 3-1 down in their last-16 tie

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Oxford plays host to a veteran film star, in town to visit a local cinema. However, a mysterious death casts a sinister pall over the occasion, and offers the newly promoted DS Endeavour Morse a fresh case to pursue. Meanwhile, a family visit puts DCI Thursday's hospitality to the test. Inspector Morse prequel, starring Shaun Evans and Roger Allam

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage seven of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 249.5km route from Vierzon to Le Creusot. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Ore Oduba, Tom Fletcher and Dougie Poynter join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave is given a bulb to grow. Popping it in his lunch box, it isn't long before he discovers it's partial to Betty's sandwiches.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: George adapts the spin cycle of the washing machine causing a black hole to form in the Spuds' kitchen.

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together

programme.title: LEGO Hidden Side
programme.desc: Jack is excited. His latest video channel upload of last night's ghost hunt has gone viral and is getting tons of vies. But what's wrong with Parker?

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike has a visitor for the afternoon - Duchess - and she's still hopelessly infatuated with Mike.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: It's a beautiful summer day and Mike decides to woo Iris with a refreshing pineapple cocktail.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The lovable halfwit unknowingly takes a monkey home in his car after visiting a safari park. After hearing on the news that the monkey has been 'stolen', Bean realises he must return him without getting into trouble

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Animated series based on and serving as a prequel to the popular film series. Preparations for the wedding of the century between Wendy's dad and Pedro's mum continue, but can Mavis find the groom in time?

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp are joined by resident chef Shivi Ramoutar, and guests Jason Donovan and Supervet Noel Fitzpatrick

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by Cliff Richard, while chefs Sat Bains and Richard Bertinet are on hand to provide a selection of recipes. James also provides a masterclass on chocolate mousse, and travels to the Scottish Highlands for his latest food adventure

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef pays culinary homage to his mother's cooking by preparing some childhood favourites, including vegetable soup three ways and an assiette of salads made with produce fresh from his gardens. He is joined by Nathan Outlaw, who serves up a dish of fish and chips with mushy peas

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The TV chef enjoys a trip to Southfork, the ranch made famous in TV soap Dallas. While in town, James visits the location of the Texas State Fair and shares his own pastiche of the world-famous drama series

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 Coral-Eclipse from Sandown Park, which follows races at 1.50 and 3.00. Plus, three races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Lancashire Oaks and 3.15 Old Newton Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman at Sandown and Sally Ann Grassick from Haydock, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the third quarter-final, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. After only finishing third in their group below England and Croatia, the Czechs produced one of the shocks of the tournament so far in their last-16 tie with the Netherlands, capitalising on Matthijs de Ligt's red card to secure a 2-0 victory courtesy of strikes by Tomas Holes and Patrik Schick. However, they now face another tough task against the Danes, who were shaken by talisman Christian Eriksen's cardiac arrest and lost their opening two matches to Iceland and Belgium, but have since gone from strength to strength, defeating Russia 4-1, before overwhelming Wales 4-0 in the last 16, as they look to emulate their triumph in Euro 92

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: Under heavy pressure from her father, Beca reluctantly starts her first year at Barden University and gets an internship at the school radio station. She soon acquires a new circle of friends in the form of a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing, joiining their a cappella group and and trying to inject some much needed energy into their repertoire. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Brittany Snow, Anna Camp, Rebel Wilson and Skylar Astin

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Action from today's quarter-finals, which were Ukraine v England at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, and Czech Republic v Denmark at Olympic Stadium in Baku

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Oprah Winfrey, Boris Johnson, Sandra Bullock, Tom Hanks, One Direction, Michael McIntyre, Dame Joan Collins, Big Narstie, Danny Dyer, James Corden, Carrie Fisher, Russell Howard and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage eight of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 151km route from Oyonnax to Le Grand-Bornand. programme.desc: Vinnie Jones joins Piers Morgan for an intimate Life Stories special, 10 years on from his first appearance on the show. Vinnie opens up about how his life has changed irrevocably in that time, as he comes to terms with grieving for the loss of his wife, Tanya, who passed away last year, after a six-year battle with cancer. He also looks back at some of the highlights of his football and movie careers, talks about his struggles with alcohol, how he is hoping to help other people, particularly men, to cope with grief and what the future now holds

There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef pays culinary homage to his mother's cooking by preparing some childhood favourites, including vegetable soup three ways and an assiette of salads made with produce fresh from his gardens. programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 Coral-Eclipse from Sandown Park, which follows races at 1.50 and 3.00. Plus, three races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Lancashire Oaks and 3.15 Old Newton Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman at Sandown and Sally Ann Grassick from Haydock, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the third quarter-final, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. After only finishing third in their group below England and Croatia, the Czechs produced one of the shocks of the tournament so far in their last-16 tie with the Netherlands, capitalising on Matthijs de Ligt's red card to secure a 2-0 victory courtesy of strikes by Tomas Holes and Patrik Schick. However, they now face another tough task against the Danes, who were shaken by talisman Christian Eriksen's cardiac arrest and lost their opening two matches to Iceland and Belgium, but have since gone from strength to strength, defeating Russia 4-1, before overwhelming Wales 4-0 in the last 16, as they look to emulate their triumph in Euro 92

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: Under heavy pressure from her father, Beca reluctantly starts her first year at Barden University and gets an internship at the school radio station. She soon acquires a new circle of friends in the form of a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing, joiining their a cappella group and and trying to inject some much needed energy into their repertoire. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Brittany Snow, Anna Camp, Rebel Wilson and Skylar Astin

programme.desc: Action from today's quarter-finals, which were Ukraine v England at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, and Czech Republic v Denmark at Olympic Stadium in Baku

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry begins his job helping Chris and it is not long before Chris is driving him round the bend. Tricia and Lyn both have the hots for Paddy's cousin Jason

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the link between trespassers on the local railway line and thefts from a fur factory, culminating in a dramatic chase on board a moving train. Meanwhile, Greengrass acquires a metal detector and thinks he has struck gold - but someone else gets to it first. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy receives confirmation that Elsa is now Alice's legal guardian. Graham consoles her and invites her out for dinner. Ashley has concerns about Bernice and Gavin's relationship

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned wants Roy and Kelly to spend Christmas with him in Ibiza. Emily is worried that her dad will spend Christmas alone and wants to invite him to the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve tells Fiona she was right to sack Maxine. Emily, Spider and Alma want to help Curly, and Kevin struggles when the child minder falls ill

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine insists that Fiona is told the truth about her and Steve. When the police search for Curly, Emily Spider and Alma cover for him. Sally begs Kevin to let her see the girls

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of a sheep farmer leads Wycliffe into the heart of a rural community whose apparently tranquil surface conceals a desperate struggle for survival. It is not long before he uncovers disturbing evidence suggesting foul play. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the residents try to return to normal life following the armistice, a new development stuns them. Meanwhile, Carlisle puts Anna in a difficult position, Bates reels from the news about Vera, and Thomas embarks on a new money-making scheme. Costume drama, starring Iain Glen, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Rob James-Collier

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The unorthodox but brilliant North East detective investigates when charred human remains are found in an abattoir furnace, and attempts to deduce how and why they got there in the first place. The bones are identified as belonging to a fellow cop, leading Vera to uncover a web of dark secrets as she looks into her dead colleague's fraud squad. Brenda Blethyn dons her familiar hat and mac to play Vera in this mystery, based on the characters created by Ann Cleeves

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: The Danish Girl (2015)
programme.desc: Artist Gerda Wegener unveils a portrait of her husband Einar dressed as a woman in 1920s Copenhagen. Einar realises that the woman in the painting is his true identity, and decides to become one of the first people to undergo sex reassignment surgery. Period drama based on David Ebershoff's novel inspired by the true story of artist Lili Elbe, starring Eddie Redmayne and an Oscar-winning Alicia Vikander

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local gamekeeper is injured in a shooting incident on the moors and suspicion falls on his disgruntled predecessor, whose 11-year-old daughter provides damaging testimony. Elsewhere, Maggie discovers her ex-husband has started work at the hospital and Greengrass schemes away as usual, risking Gina's displeasure by persuading Steve to install a pool table at the pub. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is heading for a fall, with neither Turner nor Terry willing to tolerate being ordered around by him. Ashley cancels the pantomime. Butch invites John round for Christmas

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the day of the Christmas Show, but Ashley has no star act. Zak's latest money-making scheme does not go to plan. Marc disrupts Sean and Angie's plans for a family Christmas

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally feels she cannot cope without the girls. Vera and Jack are furious when Alec docks their rent from their wages. Jim urges Fiona not to cast Steve away over just one mistake

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie helps Kevin out by looking after Sophie. Jim accuses Fiona of lying to him to get back at her for wrecking her wedding. She tells him Steve really did try to kill him

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The wedding approaches and excitement is in the air, but Branson's plans could rattle everyone. Spanish flu reaches the house and a desperate Thomas looks for a way to re-establish himself. Ethel faces a dilemma when the Bryants return with a heartbreaking proposition, and a decisive Anna forces Bates to think about their future. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Rob James-Collier, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt and Brendan Coyle

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Carry On Abroad (1972)
programme.desc: British holiday-makers jet off on a package holiday to the Spanish resort of Elsbels. Unluckily, not only is the hotel short of staff, but it also has no walls, ceilings or entertainment. Further mishaps leave the tourists fuming - until they sample a local punch laced with an aphrodisiac. Comedy, with Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family members are surprised to find out that Leslie has three different girlfriends, so Louisa decides to take it upon herself to help him pick one by having each member of the family invite their favourite girl to afternoon tea. Larry injures his leg and Theo decides to use his newly set-up x-ray unit to try and examine it. Elsewhere, Gerry continues to grow his animal collection, Margo takes up a job as Theo's assistant, and Aunt Hermione arrives in Corfu for good

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.desc: Steve asks Maxine not to tell Fiona about what happened. Sally is frustrated by the time it is taking for her to get the girls back. Curly receives a visit from the police

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is charged with trying to extort money from Freshco. Steve accuses Maxine of wrecking Fiona's life. Emily and Spider worry about Curly when he disappears

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Terry begins his job helping Chris and it is not long before Chris is driving him round the bend. Tricia and Lyn both have the hots for Paddy's cousin Jason

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice asks Tricia to be her bridesmaid. Claudia rebuffs Chris's advances. Ashley decides to stage a panto. Paddy is confused as to why Jason is flirting with Tricia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is pestered by the sister of Aidensfield's landed gentry, while Greengrass has his sights set in the same direction - poaching on the lord's land

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick investigates the link between trespassers on the local railway line and thefts from a fur factory, culminating in a dramatic chase on board a moving train. Meanwhile, Greengrass acquires a metal detector and thinks he has struck gold - but someone else gets to it first. Nick Berry and Bill Maynard star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy receives confirmation that Elsa is now Alice's legal guardian. Graham consoles her and invites her out for dinner. Ashley has concerns about Bernice and Gavin's relationship

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned wants Roy and Kelly to spend Christmas with him in Ibiza. Emily is worried that her dad will spend Christmas alone and wants to invite him to the Dingles

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve tells Fiona she was right to sack Maxine. Emily, Spider and Alma want to help Curly, and Kevin struggles when the child minder falls ill

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maxine insists that Fiona is told the truth about her and Steve. When the police search for Curly, Emily Spider and Alma cover for him. Sally begs Kevin to let her see the girls

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The mysterious disappearance of a sheep farmer leads Wycliffe into the heart of a rural community whose apparently tranquil surface conceals a desperate struggle for survival. It is not long before he uncovers disturbing evidence suggesting foul play. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: As the residents try to return to normal life following the armistice, a new development stuns them. Meanwhile, Carlisle puts Anna in a difficult position, Bates reels from the news about Vera, and Thomas embarks on a new money-making scheme. Costume drama, starring Iain Glen, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Rob James-Collier

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The unorthodox but brilliant North East detective investigates when charred human remains are found in an abattoir furnace, and attempts to deduce how and why they got there in the first place. The bones are identified as belonging to a fellow cop, leading Vera to uncover a web of dark secrets as she looks into her dead colleague's fraud squad. Brenda Blethyn dons her familiar hat and mac to play Vera in this mystery, based on the characters created by Ann Cleeves

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: A young woman is murdered in the vicinity of a library and museum owned by a wealthy philanthropist with an interest in global mysticism. It's a complicated investigation, which leads the detectives into a world of mysticism, erotica, drug addiction and tempestuous family dynamics. But as if the case were not enough to contend with, Van der Valk also finds himself fending off office gossip about himself and his newly single colleague Lucienne. Marc Warren stars as the shrewd Dutch detective, with Maimie McCoy and Luke Allen-Gale

programme.title: The Danish Girl (2015)
programme.desc: Artist Gerda Wegener unveils a portrait of her husband Einar dressed as a woman in 1920s Copenhagen. Einar realises that the woman in the painting is his true identity, and decides to become one of the first people to undergo sex reassignment surgery. Period drama based on David Ebershoff's novel inspired by the true story of artist Lili Elbe, starring Eddie Redmayne and an Oscar-winning Alicia Vikander

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Reports of attacks in a remote part of Ukraine take extreme angler Jeremy Wade on a hunt for an elusive lake monster, and to the site of the world's worst nuclear accident - Chernobyl's reactor number four. Can he reveal the culprit and deliver it to the scientists searching for evidence of mutant species?

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry panics after putting himself in an incriminating position by leaving his fingerprints on a stolen lorry full of electrical items he has collected for Arthur. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon finds himself teetering between two women racing drivers - who are rivals in love as well as on the track. Adventure, guest starring Kate O'Mara

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Two spies obtain information about a secret missile base intending to pass it to the other side via a person known as Captain Caruso, whom Steed and Mrs Peel are tasked to locate. Vintage adventure, with Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Dave's relief manager lets him down at short notice, he asks Terry to step into the breach and run the Winchester Club for him. However, concerns mount for both Terry and Arthur when the landlord fails to return as planned - especially as the club's licence is due for imminent renewal. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar tries to stop a crime ring forming a deadly alliance with psychology students who plan murders. Drama, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea The Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage five of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 27.2km individual time-trial from Changé to Laval Espace Mayenne. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Tour de France Live
programme.desc: Coverage of stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Beyond the Amazon, in the remote jungles of Guyana, fishermen are disappearing without trace. Locals blame the 'water mama', a mermaid that is said to drag victims to their death. Could the largely unexplored rivers of this region be hiding a new monster, or is there a more familiar predator lurking beneath the surface? When an eyewitness claims to have seen the creature in a lake, Jeremy Wade's hunt for this mysterious bodysnatcher takes an unexpected turn, bringing him face to face with an astonishing beast

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole tours Majorca on a classic bike, begins the restoration of a vintage Harley-Davidson, and visits Bike Shed in London, the home of the New Wave custom scene

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: The first leg of The Tour of France sees Ali, Dovey and Spooner head to the south of the country near Marseille, to a large natural lake called Carces. This tricky wild water, situated within rocky terrain, is rumoured by locals to hold several carp over the magical 70lb mark. This adventure is far from plain sailing as the team are faced with some huge physical and mental challenges - extreme weather conditions, heavy weed beds and giant catfish.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade visits Papua New Guinea, an island renowned for cannibalism, macabre rituals, headhunters and bitter tribal conflict. However, a new threat is sweeping through the region and he sets out on the trail of a creature that has reportedly been tearing chunks out of fishermen and devouring male body parts

programme.title: Die Hard with a Vengeance (1995)
programme.desc: New York cop John McClane returns to duty following an explosion at a department store in the city. The perpetrator contacts the police and tells them that unless McClane and an unfortunate bystander who was in the wrong place at the wrong time carry out a series of instructions to the letter, more bombs will be detonated. Action thriller sequel, starring Bruce Willis, Samuel L Jackson and Jeremy Irons

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Dave's relief manager lets him down at short notice, he asks Terry to step into the breach and run the Winchester Club for him. programme.title: The Avengers

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect a deposed African politician, who is due to attend a conference with a view to regaining power. After foiling two assassination attempts, they soon appreciate others share information on his whereabouts. Charles Dance and Geoffrey Palmer feature alongside stars Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes involved with a gambler, whose sudden death leaves the dodgy car dealer £2,000 worse off. Meanwhile, Terry helps his old flame Sylvie leave her abusive husband. Billy Connolly guest stars in the vintage comedy drama, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar rescues a Russian damsel in distress and agrees to help her fend off art thieves eager to get their hands on her da Vinci masterpieces. Cult adventure series, starring Roger Moore

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage six of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 161km route from Tours to Chateauroux. programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A selection of the best World Cup Goals through the years - beginning with Geoff Hurst's hat-trick for England in 1966, right up to the 2018 World Cup in Russia

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water that's sliced cleanly through a young man's testicles, is enough to lure 'fishing detective' Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade returns to Africa's Okavango Delta to reinvestigate the tigerfish - a pack-hunting carnivore previously blamed for mauling the victims of a capsized boat. If these fish are as lethal as Jeremy concluded, then why aren't there more reports of fatal, piranha-like attacks? In a bid to flush out the true nature of these sight-hunting predators, Jeremy puts on his dive gear and offers himself as live bait

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove continue their rebuild of a Harley WLC. Henry also visits the Norton factory at Donington and finds out about the iconic Sportser bike

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage seven of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 249.5km route from Vierzon to Le Creusot. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Border Force officer Rob deals with cargo trying to enter the country involving frozen chicken, and there is a surprise in store for passengers flying to Heathrow's top European destination

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Police officers are called in to deal with an emergency at one of the airport gates. Meanwhile, Ian shows off his muscles out on the airfield

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade has hunted down freshwater killers for 30 years, but only now has he discovered he was sharing those waters with darker, deadlier creatures - parasites.

programme.desc: The team responds to a farm SOS, when a skid loader slides into a dam, as well as an extreme helicopter salvage mission that requires access to a protected nature reserve

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are at Sandown Park ahead of Coral Eclipse day, another highlight of the 2021 flat season

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Three bodies from Peru, Colombia and Brazil have been recovered with mysterious injuries that look like burn marks, but who or what could have inflicted them? programme.title: Mr Bean
programme.desc: Rowan Atkinson leaves his brain at home once more to play the bungling buffoon of few words. He goes shopping for a new TV, has problems with an itchy foot and takes a lucky girl for a night on the town - leaving the usual trail of chaos in his wake

programme.title: Mr Bean

programme.desc: Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove look to make a profit on motorcycle memorabilia

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights

programme.title: Ghostbusters (1984)
programme.desc: Three eccentric scientists put their hi-tech gadgetry and knowledge of the paranormal to money-making use by setting up in business as freelance ghost catchers. A musician calls them in to investigate when she sees strange creatures in her kitchen, only to end up possessed. As the spook-hunting team members deal with an attempt to shut their operation down, they discover an evil demonic entity is being summoned into existence and it falls to them to stop this creature from destroying the world. Supernatural comedy adventure, starring Bill Murray, Dan Aykroyd, Harold Ramis, Sigourney Weaver, Ernie Hudson and Rick Moranis

However, to make the budget work, he needs to be done and dusted in 24 hours

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Ghostbusters (1984)
programme.desc: Three eccentric scientists put their hi-tech gadgetry and knowledge of the paranormal to money-making use by setting up in business as freelance ghost catchers. A musician calls them in to investigate when she sees strange creatures in her kitchen, only to end up possessed. As the spook-hunting team members deal with an attempt to shut their operation down, they discover an evil demonic entity is being summoned into existence and it falls to them to stop this creature from destroying the world. Sheila Hancock and Gyles Brandreth steer a 72-foot vessel along the waterways of Staffordshire, journeying along the Caldon Canal and the Trent and Mersey Canal, to Stoke-on-Trent. They begin their journey at Cheddleton Flint Mill, built in 1253, hearing why flint was such an essential pottery ingredient. programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a couple who plan to build their dream Georgian-style house in a small Buckinghamshire village. With a budget of £350,000, the couple hope to use mostly reclaimed materials from across Europe. However, when their summer deadline passes, it looks as if the family could be spending another winter in temporary accommodation

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Stephen supports Leah, and Alf brushes off concerns about his health, but Tane notices him struggling and offers to help at the Bait Shop

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Overexposed: Burying a Scandal (2018)
programme.desc: The teenage daughter of a sheriff goes on a date with a fellow student, who tries to force her into having sex with him by threatening to post intimate photos of her online if she does not. Thriller, starring Mary Katherine Duhon

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Andrea and Paul arrived in Spain 15 years ago and never went home. They run a show bar in La Cala and have recently closed their doors to double its size. They've wiped out their savings and invested 50,000 euros in the refurb. But tourism has been quiet this year and their potential goldmine has had to stay shut for six months. They've now got a date to reopen - but have they got their timing right? Cameras also return to Saydo Park, an enclave full of static caravans, mobile homes and Brits at the foot of the Andalusian Mountains

programme.title: Kensington Palace: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Part two of two. A study of Queen Victoria's teenage years at Kensington Palace, and Princess Diana's wardrobe, including the dress she wore to dance with John Travolta at the White House. Plus, a look at the biggest renovation Kensington has seen in years, as chandeliers are reinstalled in the King's Drawing Room for the first time in more than a century

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Week By the Sea
programme.desc: The comedian visits Southend-on-Sea in Essex, home to the world's largest pleasure pier and the famous narrow-gauge railway. Susan also creates her own ice cream sundae, takes part in a training exercise with a local lifeboat crew, and talks to Birds of a Feather star Linda Robson about why she has been coming to the town since she was a child

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Cornwall to help Sean and Janine, the owners of Northshore, Bude's only hostel. Having returned from travelling the world, the pair bought the property to keep their travelling dream alive. Running the hostel is the best job Sean's ever had - but a shocking downturn in occupancy and their unorthodox living scenario have left Janine wanting out. Desperate to sort the business, the couple call on Alex's advice in a last-ditch attempt to resolve matters. The expert arrives with a few surprises up her sleeve, but quickly learns saving this business isn't going to be easy

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agents Karen and Gemma investigate claims of vandalism and anti-social behaviour at a property they manage in Wigan, but when they discover the tenant is no longer living there, they track her down to learn that she's abandoned the property and has tried to move in somewhere else by forging a reference. Meanwhile, in Darwen, letting agent Paul issues proceedings on a tenant who is more than £3,000 in arrears, but Paul's attempts at evicting him are delayed when the tenant refuses to leave without Paul giving him a good reference

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera

programme.title: Spectacular Spain with Alex Polizzi
programme.desc: Alex discovers the history, art and culture of Madrid, and takes a tour of the famous Royal Palace before visiting the equally amazing former home of a Spanish aristocrat. She then heads south to the small but picturesque town of Consuegra, whose windmills inspired some of the anecdotes in Don Quixote by Miguel de Cervantes. Finally, she goes to Salamanca, a Unesco World Heritage site 110 miles north of Madrid, and admires its golden sandstone buildings, stunning architecture and busy colonnaded square

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas presents a whistle-stop tour of awkward, embarrassing and cringeworthy holiday moments, as related by a selection of comedians and celebrities. Johnny's famous contributors skewer the traditional staycation, questioning the perks of rain-drenched British beaches, and whether anyone really likes going camping. Turning their attention further afield, the stars share some of their most memorable holiday tales, including transforming a hotel corridor into one long 'slip-and-slide', the suspicious discovery of a pair of chicken fillets in a swimming pool in Marbella, and a blush-inducing incident involving a naked paragliding session along a Spanish beachfront

programme.title: Scotland: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Summer has finally arrived in Scotland, bringing long days and an abundance of food. On the west coast, two white-tailed eaglets are learning to fly, and the larger female is beginning to master it. On a beach in the Shetland Islands, the seas are full of plentiful feeding opportunities for young otters learning to go it alone

programme.desc: This episode examines the story behind an iconic image created by arguably the most famous and influential artist of the 20th century, Pablo Picasso. Andrew Marr tells the harrowing and uplifting tale of his 'barbed-wire face-mask' of a painting, Weeping Woman

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 90s Songs
programme.desc: Gabrielle, Blur's Alex James, Boyzone's Shane Lynch, Steps' Lee Latchford-Evans, and Hannah Spearritt from S Club 7 share their memories of pop stardom in 1996. Shaun Ryder, Tito Jackson, Terry Christian, Michaela Strachan, Rhona Cameron, Rowland Rivron, Toby Anstis and other famous faces guide viiewers through the biggest hits of the year. The Spice Girls hit the big time, Oasis were still riding high in the charts, and Babylon Zoo had a memorable smash with Spaceman, but who had the biggest-selling single of the year?

programme.title: 13 Moments...
programme.desc: An extraordinary musician with an unforgettable voice, Amy Winehouse's meteoric rise saw her blossom into a truly global star who changed the landscape of modern pop culture. However, the world also witnessed her drug and alcohol addiction, dramatic weight loss and involvement in a destructive relationship. These factors led to her premature death in July 2011, aged 27. This film features contributions from some of those who knew Amy well and those who tried to help her, telling the story of a towering but troubled talent, through the chain of key moments that led ultimately towards tragedy

programme.title: Spectacular Spain with Alex Polizzi
programme.desc: The presenter discovers a lush, mountainous region in the north-west with a very different climate and culture from the rest of Spain. In many ways Galicia resembles Ireland rather than southern Europe and after visiting its rugged stretch of shoreline notorious for its many shipwrecks, Alex meets Mari Carmen, who scrapes the prized local delicacy of gooseneck barnacles off the rocks to sell. Alex also explores the ancient forests of Ponferrada and Galicia's Celtic past, and makes her way to the pilgrim city of Santiago de Compostela, where she visits a martial arts school

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Ritz ranks high among London's most legendary hotels, one that has been synonymous with luxury and wealth, as well as being a bastion of privilege for over 100 years. Situated on Piccadilly, just a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, it has been a home from home for the royal family since the reign of Edward VII. The Ritz has redefined service in luxury hotels, introducing the now-universal concept of the customer always being right. This programme examines its heritage, its famous afternoon teas and the scandalous history of its founder, Cesar Ritz

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice helps a widower sell his 200-year-old cottage so he can pack up and start a new life in Scotland. The property is basically sound, but has an air of bachelor neglect made worse by the owner's pet Labrador, which acts like it owns the place

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Estate agent Ann Maurice offers her expertise to a couple whose home in Rothwell near Leeds is still for sale after 10 months, despite having attracted more than 30 viewings. programme.desc: Phoebe encounters a suicidal office worker during her first day as a telemarketer. Ross and Monica are upset to discover their parents intend to sell their childhood home, while Rachel and Chandler blame themselves for breaking Joey's favourite chair

programme.desc: The pals celebrate Rachel's 30th birthday and reminisce about their own, including Monica's attempt to hide her intoxication from her parents, and Ross's embarrassing crisis, which resulted in the purchase of a red sports car. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and David Schwimmer

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey tells a fellow soap cast member that her character is to be killed off. programme.title: Friends
