programme.title: The Wonderful World of Crafting
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rosie Wolfenden follow as three hobby crafters present their works for the first time at Brancepeth Castle Craft Fair in Durham. Occupational therapist Olivia dyes her own yarn and produces hand-woven pieces, ex-builder Neil makes quirky metal ornaments and Wendi now makes her own glass pieces uses the art of fusing - and for the fair she even learns the art of glass blowing

programme.title: The World's Most Expensive Cruise Ships
programme.desc: This behind-the-scenes look at life on board the Seven Seas Explorer features head sommelier Georgiana, as the vessel prepares for a special visit to Bordeaux. Cameras go below deck and take a peek at the unique connoisseur drinks cellar with its £3,000 bottle of cognac and observes members of the night crew as they work to get the ship looking as good as new for the morning

programme.title: Elephant Hospital
programme.desc: The work of a Thailand hospital devoted solely to treating elephants. The two-part documentary reveals the day-to-day challenges of dealing with supersized patients suffering everything from deadly viruses to cuts and parasites. It also shows how the facility teaches baby elephants new commands and even turns elephant poo into fertiliser

programme.title: Elephant Hospital
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Paul O'Donoghue joins the expert vets in Thailand at the world's largest hospital devoted solely to treating elephants. A night-time emergency stretches the hospital as three sick female elephants from the same sanctuary are admitted with colic. One of the patients arrives with her four-month-old baby, who stays while her mother receives treatment, but it proves to be a massive task to nurse these big patients back to health

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: All nine children return home for Christmas. Clemmy has her hands full nursing a premature calf, while Clive has fixed up an ancient contraption to cook the festive roast. When an owl is sighted in one of the barns, the family comes up with a plan to encourage more birds to roost there. Miles rigs up a night vision system to keep an eye on their guest, while Sidney traps pests to lure it out

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: The family has recently discovered that their home is more than 700 years old, which inspires Edith and Violet to search the barn for evidence of previous occupants. Clemmy helps Amanda to treat a ewe suffering from a nasty ailment, while the discovery of a collection of toy steam trains sparks a restoration project for Clive and the boys

programme.title: William & Harry: Princes at War?
programme.desc: As speculation heats up around the relationship between Prince Harry and Prince William, this programme assesses the truth behind the rumours. The two princes have grown up in the media spotlight and are rumoured to have grown apart. They now have separate charities, split social media accounts, different staff and two households, and there's analysis of what the future holds for both of them

programme.title: Elizabeth II & the Traitor King: A Secret Friendship
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of King Edward VIII, told through the prism of his turbulent relationship with his niece, Princess Elizabeth, who ultimately became Queen Elizabeth II. Throughout the programme, the twists and turns of Edward and Elizabeth's complicated relationship are given full exploration, and experts contrast the artistic license of such dramas as The Crown with pivotal moments from their relationship that are known to be true

programme.title: When Classic TV Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Less-than-perfect moments from some of the nation's favourite shows over the years. From the black and white days of the 1960s and Dixon of Dock Green, to the 1970s and The Generation Game, right through the 80s, 90s and 00s to the present day. There are disastrous moments from classic stars, including Lenny Henry, Lorraine Kelly, Oliver Reed and Russell Harty. Narrated by Chris Tarrant

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Catching up with former dancer and onsite sales supremo Bambi, who is working harder than ever in her role as part of the caravan sales team. She welcomes British couple Alan and Caroline to their new static home, and also gives viewers a peek inside her own luxury caravan. What her neighbours don't know is that plans for a new caravan park are afoot, and Bambi's bosses have asked her to relocate. Narrated by John Thomson

programme.title: Inside Tesco: 24/7
programme.desc: The retailer's transformation from a cut-price upstart to market leader. After the death of its founder Jack Cohen in 1979, the company was in the middle of a high-risk transition and an outside takeover was a real danger. Ian MacLaurin, Jack Cohen's first management trainee, was chief executive at the time. His vision for the company was very different from Jack's pile-it-high, sell-it cheap approach. He set out to transform the company and compete with the number one retailer Sainsbury's - something that was achieved in 1995

programme.title: House Busters
programme.desc: Janet Ellis meets a homeowner who feels trapped in her property, and has seen various attempts to sell it fall through. She receives advice from a feng shui expert, who claims she is too fixated on the past, a 'home healer', who diagnoses low energy levels in the property, and a mystic who announces that the tree outside her bedroom window has to go

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Interior design expert Ann Maurice visits Stepney in London's East End, where she advises part-time DJ Simon Denny on how to make his two-bedroom split-level converted brewery lodge more attractive to potential buyers

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mr Potato visits the playgroup with his superhero friend Super Potato, who teaches the children the importance of eating fruit and vegetables every day

programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey's Amazing Adventures
programme.desc: Monkey visits Edinburgh, Scotland's capital city, where he learns about traditional Scottish clothing and music

programme.title: When Eight Bells Toll (1971)
programme.desc: A Navy intelligence agent heads to Scotland, where he poses as a marine biologist to investigate the theft of millions of pounds in gold bullion from ships off the British coast. However, the locals counter his enquiries with hostility, especially when he sets his sights on a Greek tycoon, whom he suspects may be operating as a modern-day pirate. Thriller, with Anthony Hopkins, Robert Morley and Jack Hawkins. Based on a novel by Alistair MacLean

programme.title: Destroyer (1943)
programme.desc: A former welder working on the Navy destroyer whose construction he oversaw angers his crewmates with his relentless quest for perfection. Wartime adventure, starring Edward G Robinson, Glenn Ford, Marguerite Chapman and Regis Toomey

programme.title: Tomahawk (1951)
programme.desc: Frontier scout Jim Bridger doesn't approve when a gold rush leads the US army to build a fort on land occupied by the Sioux. The situation goes from bad to worse when a sadistic officer launches an unprovoked attack, prompting a vengeful tribe to go on the offensive and leaving Bridger the only one who can save the soldiers at the fort from certain death. Western, starring Van Heflin, Yvonne de Carlo and Alex Nicol, and featuring an early appearance by Rock Hudson

programme.title: Run Silent, Run Deep (1958)
programme.desc: A commander aboard a US Navy submarine has a titanic clash of egos with his second in command as the senior officer's obsession with sinking as many enemy vessels as possible, whatever the risk, spirals out of control. Second World War drama, starring Clark Gable, Burt Lancaster, Jack Warden, Brad Dexter and Don Rickles

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015)
programme.desc: The civilisation of Panem is consumed by a bloody civil war. Reluctant rebel leader Katniss Everdeen leads a band of her allies on a mission to end the conflict at one stroke by assassinating the despotic President Snow - but faces many dangers as she closes in on her target. Conclusion of the sci-fi adventure series based on Suzanne Collins' novels, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Donald Sutherland and Philip Seymour Hoffman in his final role

programme.title: The Equalizer (2014)
programme.desc: A retired secret agent applies his talents to the task of helping victims of injustice. When he witnesses a prostitute being beaten up by a pimp, he kills the man, an act that draws him into a battle to the death with a Russian crime syndicate. Action thriller based on the 1980s TV series, starring Denzel Washington and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Hear My Song (1991)
programme.desc: The owner of a struggling nightclub claims he will host a performance by the legendary reclusive tenor Josef Locke. He ends up booking an imposter, but when the ruse is exposed, he faces the wrath of both his girlfriend and the entire community, so heads to Ireland to redeem himself by persuading the real Locke to come out of retirement for one last show. Fictional comedy based on a real-life incident, starring Adrian Dunbar and Ned Beatty

programme.title: Stratton (2017)
programme.desc: A British Special Boat Service commando tracks down an international terrorist cell. Thriller, starring Dominic Cooper, Austin Stowell and Gemma Chan

programme.title: The Long Memory (1952)
programme.desc: A man is released from prison after serving a 12-year sentence for a crime he did not commit, and immediately sets about tracking down three witnesses who lied in court, bringing about his conviction. However, while seeking revenge, he discovers the case is more complicated than expected. Thriller, starring John Mills, Elizabeth Sellars, John McCallum and Geoffrey Keen

programme.title: Shenandoah (1965)
programme.desc: A Virginian farmer is left to raise seven children after his wife dies. He tries to keep his family out of the American Civil War, but when Union troops abduct his son, and his son-in-law enlists with the Confederates, he is unable to maintain his neutral stance and is dragged into the conflict. Western, starring James Stewart, Doug McClure, Rosemary Forsyth, Katharine Ross and Patrick Wayne

programme.title: Star Trek Special
programme.desc: Leonard Nimoy and Jonathan Frakes discuss their roles as both actors and directors in the long-running sci-fi series

programme.title: Hatari! (1962)
programme.desc: A group of reckless animal trappers travels through the wilds of Africa, but the arrival of a beautiful photographer looks set to cause upheaval among the group. Comedy adventure, starring John Wayne, Red Buttons, Hardy Kruger, Elsa Martinelli and Michele Girardon

programme.title: Star Trek: The Motion Picture (1979)
programme.desc: A mysterious, planet-sized energy cloud is heading for Earth, destroying everything in its path. Admiral James T Kirk returns to the bridge of the Enterprise to stop the invader, but he has to contend with a bitter rival and the possession of one of his crew by the alien force. Sci-fi adventure, the first movie based on the TV series, starring William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, DeForest Kelley and Persis Khambatta

programme.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan (1982)
programme.desc: The Enterprise crew faces a bitter battle to the death with an old enemy. Khan, a genetically engineered superhuman tyrant, has escaped exile on a barren planet and hijacked a starship to take his revenge on Admiral Kirk, while also scheming to turn an experimental planet-creating device into a doomsday weapon. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, Ricardo Montalban and Kirstie Alley

programme.title: Star Trek III: The Search for Spock (1984)
programme.desc: Admiral Kirk and his crew hijack the decommissioned Enterprise to recover the body of their fallen comrade Mr Spock from the planet Genesis. Kirk has learnt that Spock's spirit is still alive, and the Vulcan can be resurrected, but he must also battle a Klingon captain determined to control the planet's mysterious energy. Sci-fi sequel, directed by Leonard Nimoy, with William Shatner, DeForest Kelley and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home (1986)
programme.desc: The Enterprise crew travels back in time to prevent an alien probe destroying the Earth. The only thing that can avert the disaster is a humpback whale, a creature extinct in the 23rd century - but adjusting to life back in 1986 proves a distinct challenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring William Shatner, DeForest Kelley and Leonard Nimoy, who also directed

programme.title: Star Trek V: The Final Frontier (1989)
programme.desc: The Starship Enterprise races to the rescue of intergalactic ambassadors held hostage on a distant planet. The crisis proves to be the handiwork of a renegade Vulcan mystic who kidnaps Captain Kirk and his crew, ordering them to fly to an unexplored world where he hopes to fulfil his insane ambition - to meet God. Sci-fi adventure, written, directed by and starring William Shatner, with Leonard Nimoy, DeForest Kelley and David Warner

programme.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country (1991)
programme.desc: Captain Kirk is framed for the assassination of the Klingon head of state just as the Federation is about to make peace with their old enemies. As Kirk and Dr McCoy are banished to an icy prison planet, Spock tries to uncover the identity of the real killers. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with William Shatner, Leonard Nimoy, DeForest Kelley, Christopher Plummer, with appearances by Christian Slater and Michael Dorn from Star Trek: The Next Generation

programme.title: Star Trek: Generations (1994)
programme.desc: USS Enterprise Captain Jean-Luc Picard must stop a deranged genius obsessed with gaining access to a mysterious energy source - regardless of the threat to entire civilisations that lie in his path. Help arrives from an unlikely quarter - his supposedly long dead predecessor Captain James T Kirk. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Patrick Stewart, William Shatner, Malcolm McDowell and Whoopi Goldberg

programme.title: Star Trek: First Contact (1996)
programme.desc: Captain Jean-Luc Picard and the crew of the Enterprise face their deadliest enemies, the Borg, who travel back in time in an attempt to change Earth's history. The starship follows on a mission to foil their plan, but the vessel is taken over by members of the cyborg collective, leaving Picard consumed by a desire for revenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Patrick Stewart, Jonathan Frakes, Brent Spiner, James Cromwell and Alice Krige

programme.title: Star Trek: Insurrection (1998)
programme.desc: Captain Picard and the Enterprise crew uncover a conspiracy by their own superiors and a sinister alien race to take over a peaceful world and exploit its power to prolong life. However, the crew is forced to defy Federation orders and turns renegade to protect the planet's inhabitants. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Patrick Stewart, F Murray Abraham, Jonathan Frakes, Brent Spiner and Michael Dorn

programme.title: Watchmen (2009)
programme.desc: At the height of the Cold War, former superheroes driven into retirement by public outcry are confronted with the murder of one of their number. As they face the possibility of a conspiracy to eliminate them, they must contend with a world edging closer to the brink of nuclear war. Sci-fi drama, based on Alan Moore and Dave Gibbons' graphic novel, starring Patrick Wilson, Jackie Earle Haley, Malin Akerman and Billy Crudup

programme.title: The Devil's Doorway (2018)
programme.desc: In the autumn of 1960, Father Thomas Riley and Father John Thornton are sent by the Vatican to investigate a miraculous event in an Irish home for 'fallen women', only to uncover something much more terrifying. Horror, starring Lalor Roddy, Ciaran Flynn and Helena Bereen

programme.title: My Life as a Courgette (2016)
programme.desc: After losing his mother, a young boy is sent to a foster home with other orphans his age, where he begins to learn the meaning of trust and true love. Animation, with the voices of Gaspard Schlatter, Sixtine Murat and Paulin Jaccoud

programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

Gordon's help is urgently needed to get customers back through the door, but the restaurateur does not take kindly to criticism and closes the restaurant down when his employees turn on him

programme.title: Baby Daddy

Meanwhile, Ryder has problems approaching a new girl and Joe tries to give him advice. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence

programme.title: Melissa & Joey

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Raj turn to Bernadette for advice about their jealousy when Amy and Howard decide to collaborate on a new project. Penny and Leonard learn some new ways to manage Sheldon's irksome behaviour from an unlikely source

programme.desc: Sheldon competes with Wil Wheaton (guest starring as himself) for the role of the new Professor Proton when the scientist's old TV series gets a reboot. Penny looks after Halley when Howard and Bernadette are both put on bed rest

programme.desc: Dre chaperones the twins' school dance to keep an eye on Jack, who keeps getting into trouble. Junior's girlfriend thinks he spends too much time taking care of Devante

programme.desc: Dre's sister Rhonda takes Jack and Diane to her part of town in a black neighbourhood and claims the twins are scared of their own community. Rainbow falls into the trap of becoming the 'office mom' at work, so Andre Jr tries to help her break the label

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom Calvin plans an over-the-top Scottish wedding at Loch Ness, but his bride-to-be Leanne has always dreamed of a simple affair

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Bride-to-be Jenna could be in for a scary surprise as her fiance Nathan is planning a Mexican day of the dead themed wedding for their big day

programme.title: Supernanny US
programme.desc: Ben Corry is away for months at a time with the army, leaving stay-at-home mum Maria to discipline their four rowdy daughters. Jo Frost brings this military family into line

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: The Lobdells take on the Moons as a no-nonsense fire chief, who attempts to run her house like her battalion, swaps lives with a conservative, stay-at-home wife who caters to the every whim of her domineering husband

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Denver, Colorado, where he meets the stubborn owner of Italian restaurant Pantaleone's, who appears to be firmly stuck in the past. Pete maintains he makes the best pizza in town - based on a review from the 1980s - but the fiery chef's own taste test proves the opposite. With Pete's wife Paulette and son Josh threatening to leave, can Gordon get through to him and save the business?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Woodland Park, Colorado, where guests have a choice between casual fine-dining service or a pick-up from the drive-through takeaway. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point

programme.title: The Spiderwick Chronicles (2008)
programme.desc: Life for identical twins Jared and Simon and their sister Mallory begins afresh when their mother inherits a spooky mansion. But after moving in, strange things begin to happen, and as the youngsters investigate, they discover a book that opens up a mysterious world of amazing supernatural creatures. Fantasy adventure, starring Freddie Highmore in a dual role alongside Sarah Bolger and Nick Nolte

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the comedy about scientists and their friendship with a down-to-earth neighbour. When aspiring actress Penny moves into Leonard's apartment block, he is immediately smitten, but Sheldon warns him that he runs the risk of a broken heart if he tries to woo her. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon helps Leonard drop off a parcel for Penny, but when he sees how messy her flat is, he tries to do a good deed - which is not well received. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is upset to learn Penny is dating someone, so his friends encourage him to seek a new romance by asking another woman out. Sara Gilbert appears alongside her Roseanne co-star Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is fired for being disrespectful to his boss, and explores new activities in a bid to find a purpose in life other than physics, but Leonard takes drastic action to restore him to his old self. Laurie Metcalf guest stars as Sheldon's mother

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard fears he is wasting his time with Penny and ends up spending the night with fellow scientist Leslie. However, he is disappointed when she later tells him she was only interested in a one-night stand. Meanwhile, Sheldon frets over a change to his hamburger routine. Starring Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny hosts a Halloween costume party, which proves a worthy test for the boys' social skills. During the event, Leonard has a run-in with Penny's ex-boyfriend. Kaley Cuoco, Johnny Galecki and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang become concerned when Sheldon's former admirer, Dr Ramona Nowitzki, resurfaces while Amy is away working at Princeton University. Jim Parsons stars

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee (1986)
programme.desc: A New York reporter searches the Australian Outback for a maverick hunter with a legendary reputation, and invites the adventurer back to America. But the die-hard Aussie needs to draw on all his survival skills to adjust to life in the urban jungle. Comedy adventure, written by and starring Paul Hogan, with Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Mark Blum

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Shows including Countdown, Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure, Titanic, First Dates and Serengeti come under the critical gaze of famous faces including Jessie J, Jamal Edwards, Martin and Roman Kemp, Rylan Clark-Neal, Denise Van Outen, Oti Mabuse, Chris Eubank Sr, Chris Eubank Jr, Stanley Johnson, Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo, Laurence and Emilia Fox, Gyles Brandreth, Sheila Hancock, Emily Atack, Kate Robbins, Nick Grimshaw and Olly Alexander

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoughts on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, including Tom Allen's Quizness, Mare of Easttown, Gregg Wallace's Big Weekend Away, Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again, Dung Dynasty, Running Wild with Bear Grylls, The Andrew Marr Show, and ITV News

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The nation's favourite armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during October, 2016, including The Apprentice, National Treasure, Terminator 2, Celebrity Island with Bear Grylls, The X Factor and the News

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Rick battles with the sinister owner of the second-hand shop where Summer has a job, but Summer is upset about his interference. Jerry helps Morty with his science project about the solar system, but they can't agree on Pluto's planetary status. Guest starring Alfred Molina, Nolan North and Rich Fulcher

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: When 27 Ricks from alternate dimensions are murdered and their respective Mortys are kidnapped, Rick is framed for committing the crimes and must go after culprit - resulting in a fall-out with Morty

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: When Beth and Jerry head off on a romantic Titanic-themed getaway, Rick and Summer throw a huge intergalactic house party that rapidly gets out of control, despite Morty's best efforts to keep things under control

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: After a delay of six months, Rick unfreezes time hoping that everything will return to normal, but warns Morty and Summer not to touch Jerry and Beth in case unstable timelines cause parallel dimensions to develop. However, the youngsters' feelings of uncertainty fracture time and split reality, leaving them lost in oblivion with Rick

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Shows including Countdown, Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure, Titanic, First Dates and Serengeti come under the critical gaze of famous faces including Jessie J, Jamal Edwards, Martin and Roman Kemp, Rylan Clark-Neal, Denise Van Outen, Oti Mabuse, Chris Eubank Sr, Chris Eubank Jr, Stanley Johnson, Georgia 'Toff' Toffolo, Laurence and Emilia Fox, Gyles Brandreth, Sheila Hancock, Emily Atack, Kate Robbins, Nick Grimshaw and Olly Alexander

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom Calvin plans an over-the-top Scottish wedding at Loch Ness, but his bride-to-be Leanne has always dreamed of a simple affair

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Bride-to-be Jenna could be in for a scary surprise as her fiance Nathan is planning a Mexican day of the dead themed wedding for their big day

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates a potentially devastating by-product of Greek yoghurt production

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers try to solve the tricky case of a man named Smith. Plus, the story of a man whose troubled childhood led him to become separated from his adoptive family, and details of more unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Henry Cole visit a motorbike fan, making table lamps from engines and turning a pallet and iron wheels into a stylish modern coffee table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head east to Newark in search of their latest upcycling prospects. Simon finds an antique European threshing board, which he turns into a coffee table, and Henry's all at sea with a ship's porthole

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend travel to Essex, where they rescue a 1974 Chevrolet Corvette Stingray that has been off the road for years. However, they soon discover it may be a bigger job than they anticipated when they get the iconic US vehicle back to the workshop

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Guests check into an unfinished holiday home, a power cut leads to dinner in the dark for restaurant owners in France and a couple of newlyweds search for a new home in Spain

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to the Borgue Hotel in Dumfries and Galloway, which owners Vince and Shirley Barrett run with military precision. On arrival, guests Adam and Naomi are not impressed with the decor, while Ken and Elaine do not like the orange towels. Later, over dinner, the hosts reveal who wears the trousers in their relationship, before Ken's jokes go down like a lead balloon

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Allscott Mill B&B in Telford, Shropshire, on their second night, where hosts Ken and Elaine Purchase welcome their guests. On arrival, Shirely and Vince find fault with the lack of headroom in their lodgings, while Adam and Naomi are bemused by the absence of a toilet brush in their bathroom

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to Park House in Llandrindod Wells, Powys, where owners Ian and Joan Barr hope their colourful B&B will impress their guests. Adam and Naomi are wowed by the vibrant decor, but Ken and Elaine take issue with a dirty toilet brush

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Naomi and Adam Black welcome the contestants to East Thorne yurt retreat in Bude, Cornwall. They are pleased to discover it is Vince and Shirley's dream location, but Ken and Elaine don't share the same opinion. Later, the guests take part in an archery competition, and in the morning, are unimpressed with the DIY breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival owners meet for the final time on the last day of the competition to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Hosting skills, breakfasts, facilities and two toilet brush gripes are aired before the cash is counted and the winner is announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Creating a 50-metre-long castle wall is just the first of many uphill battles a brave family faces as its members tackle the gargantuan task of creating a medieval castle in north Canterbury

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Two-year-old Labrador Ira is fitted with a bionic spine, while anxious owner Rob brings in eight-year-old French bulldog Tom, who has a long list of chronic ailments. Matt and Rebecca arrive at the clinic with their three-year-old cat Marley, who may need to have a leg amputated

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. Dick also converts a stone barn into a flat for Angel's parents, getting rid of 150 years' worth of rubbish in the process, but tensions rise between the couple when he gets carried away with the trees at the front of the property

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Devon for an update on an inspirational build. Royal Marine captain Jon White and his wife Becky were living happily in a small cottage when in June 2010, while serving in Afghanistan, Jon stepped on an improvised explosive device, losing three limbs. Every part of their lives had to change - starting with where they could live. Their architect came up with a cutting-edge, semi open-plan building with a dramatic wing-like-roof - and crucially no obvious signs of adaptations for Jon's disabilities. Has the new home proved as enabling for the couple and their expanding family as they had hoped?

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Dubbed 'The Beast' by Winston Churchill, Tirpitz was Nazi Germany's most powerful battleship. Protected by an elite squadron of fighters, it was seen as invincible, but after 34 failed attempts to sink it, the RAF sent in the 617 'Dambusters' Squadron and 9 Squadron. In a raid in November 1944 they managed to slip through enemy defences and destroy Tirpitz. With eyewitness accounts from surviving RAF crew, one of the Luftwaffe fighter pilots and German sailors, this documentary tells this extraordinary story and offers new evidence to explain the mission's improbable success

programme.title: World's Greatest Shipwrecks: History Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: The fate of German armed military transport ship MV Wilhelm Gustloff, which was sunk in January 1945 by Soviet submarine S-13 in the Baltic Sea while evacuating German civilian refugees. Plus, the attack on Japan's naval base at Micronesia's Chuuk Lagoon in February 1944

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old George is airlifted to St George's after he is struck by a motorcycle on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned about the extensive damage to his leg, which could lead to permanent disability. Meanwhile, 26-year-old Tanveer has come off his moped at speed, suffering a serious head injury that has left him with no memory of the event. In paediatrics, nine-year-old Amber is admitted, suffering severe stomach pains and vomiting, with doctors worried it could be appendicitis

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and Phil Wang take on guest captain Alan Carr and Harriet Kemsley. David O'Doherty and Joe Wilkinson join wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Warwick, a 46-year-old man is paralysed from his neck down six weeks after major spinal surgery. Paramedic David Hastie and student paramedic Esme Jenkins need to move him with extreme care: one false move could mean he will never walk again. At the same time, paramedics in Birmingham treat a man who has chest pains after being involved in a hit-and-run incident

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Dubbed 'The Beast' by Winston Churchill, Tirpitz was Nazi Germany's most powerful battleship. Protected by an elite squadron of fighters, it was seen as invincible, but after 34 failed attempts to sink it, the RAF sent in the 617 'Dambusters' Squadron and 9 Squadron. In a raid in November 1944 they managed to slip through enemy defences and destroy Tirpitz. With eyewitness accounts from surviving RAF crew, one of the Luftwaffe fighter pilots and German sailors, this documentary tells this extraordinary story and offers new evidence to explain the mission's improbable success

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Scotland to find out about a mysterious condition threatening salmon stocks, Kate Quilton heads Down Under to learn about kangaroo farming and Matt Tebbutt goes behind the scenes of the annual race to get new season asparagus onto the nation's supermarket shelves

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco to uncover the secrets of saffron, revealing what makes it the most expensive spice in the world

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate researchers investigate a huge case that has them searching far and wide for rightful heirs. They also successfully reunite a family separated by wartime evacuation. Plus, details of unclaimed estates

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien converts tank parts to garden furniture, while Henry Cole takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard. Later, Simon restores a model Hurricane, and Henry goes potty for a retro petrol pump

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to a private railway, where Simon converts a lantern to a stylish table and Henry turns a fire extinguisher into a steampunk lamp

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Presenter Tim Shaw, master mechanic Fuzz Townshend and a team of experts head to Eastbourne in East Sussex to take on a French front-wheel drive car. The vehicle in question is a 1954 Citroën Traction Avant belonging to former watch-maker and classic-car lover Andrew

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras return as the season comes to an end to see whether a couple of newlyweds have made a success of their new Spanish bar. Plus, a couple from Surrey consider branching out into the B&B business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off in Worcestershire, where friends Geri Atterbury and Lorraine Rigby invite guests to stay at Greenways of Malvern. On arrival, couple Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are not impressed by the wild wallpaper, and the lack of an en-suite dismays mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Royal Forester at Bewdley in Worcestershire for their second stay, with proprietors Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker hoping to impress their guests with a trip to a local safari park. Sarah and Luke are impressed with their colourful room, but Sanjeev and Elinor have a 'hairy' experience

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: From Ferry Lodge in Holyhead, Anglesey, where mother-and-son duo Sarah and Luke Allaway hope to impress the other contestants. However, things do not get off to a good start when Geri and Lorraine are overwhelmed by the number of laminated signs, Sean and Maxine begrudge paying 50p for toiletries and Sanjeev and Elinor are left unimpressed by the cleanliness of the establishment

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Partners Sanjeev Bhagotra and Elinor Roberts welcome their guests to Court Colman Manor, Bridgend, Glamorgan. Sean McGahern and Maxine Parker are pleased with the size of their room on arrival, but Sarah Allaway discovers a collection of cobwebs in hers. During the afternoon, the groups take a lesson in fire-making, and the next morning a traditional Welsh breakfast is not to everyone's taste - with the hosts wondering whether tactical voting has played a part come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet one last time to find out what they have been paid. Geri kicks off proceedings by demanding to know why Sarah and Luke did not want a return visit to Greenways of Malvern, before they in turn confront Sanjeev and Elinor over their low cleanliness score for Ferry Lodge. Then the Court Colman Manor proprietors accuse Sarah and Luke of unfairly judging their hotel, before the payments are revealed and the winners are announced

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: Auckland couple Bridget and Steve Varney take on the challenge of converting the old Farmers Grand Tearooms built in 1930 into a New York-style loft

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie discover their beloved six-year-old German shepherd has a tumour. In desperation, they turn to Noel Fitzpatrick in the hope he can find a way to prolong Otto's life until their wedding day. The team also sees Jane, Simon and their Rottweiler puppy Elvis, who is having trouble walking, and seven-week-old Pomeranian Gucci, brought in with a broken paw by Amber and her two children after only 48 hours in her new home

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. As if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows a project to renovate and extend a classic 1920s cinema in Thorne, South Yorkshire. With little knowledge of restoration, Gwyn and Kate boldly set about turning the dilapidated building into a family home using masses of concrete after taking advice from an architect friend. The couple are hoping to create a hydraulic glass wall that opens up one side of the house to form a UV-filtered canopy, and there are also plans for a white roof extension that resembles a diving board

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Coventry, crewmates Mark Rattray and Orlaigh Barbour attend a 73-year-old woman who is in agony after falling on the ice. Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy are sent to a baby who has been fitting. On the other side of the city, Neil Vann and Laura Perkins have to deal with a well-known drunk nuisance caller

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Victoria Coren Mitchell in the words-and-numbers quiz. Doctor-turned-writer Adam Kay joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Junior doctor Silas works his first ever shift in resus, and is part of the team brought in to treat a biker who is rushed to A&E after riding into a lamppost. A two-year-old boy is treated for injuries resulting from a fall, and his mother shares the story of how her eldest son was recently attacked by a gang

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A look at the night shift in a busy A&E department. A woman with a severe form of autism is brought in, having suffered an injury to one of her fingers. While she is treated, her sister shares stories of their childhood and the challenges their family have faced. An 82-year-old patient has difficulty breathing, and doctors suspect he has developed life-threatening sepsis and need to act fast to try to halt the spread of infection

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty takes to the skies above Israel as he joins the battle to protect the bell pepper crop from the Mediterranean fruit fly and explores whether dishwasher salt is suitable to put on chips. Kate Quilton visits Vietnam to find out if coconut oil is any healthier than other oils and heads to the Amazon rainforest to investigate rumours that the Brazil nuts on UK supermarket shelves are all radioactive

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty finds out that shellfish consumed by millions have been feeding on plastic discarded in the sea, with shocking global implications. Kate Quilton finds out whether the makers of Marmite can really predict whether people will love or hate their distinctive yeast-based spread, as claimed in their latest TV advert, and Tebbutt aims to solve the age-old mystery of how to smoothly extract ketchup from a bottle - by running across a pond of custard

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Warren and Jacqui Sumner from Manchester first visited Crete on their honeymoon 23 years ago and have been going back ever since. They've fallen in love with the island and are now determined to buy their very own holiday home there. Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their £75,000 budget, but will any of them be the romantic getaway they've been dreaming of?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Best friends Alison Wright and Sheila Sagar want to go halves on a holiday home in Costa Rica to escape the British winters. Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their £300,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: With their five children all grown up, Maria and Laky Zervudachi want to downsize and find two new properties to call home. They want a family home close to their favourite town of Lewes in Sussex, so that friends and a community are nearby, and a smaller apartment in sunny Palma in Mallorca so that Maria can practise yoga outdoors all year round. Jasmine Harman shows them three impressive family properties for their budget of £900,000 in and around Lewes and Jonnie Irwin lines up three possible apartments for £300,000 in Palma

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face chocolate week, beginning with a sharing dessert using both chocolate and a favourite ingredient of judge Cherish Finden. For the showpiece, the teams must construct chocolate showpieces on a house-of-horrors theme, including a moving part, along with 48 smaller treats - all in just five hours. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but she's hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the £1,000 prize. She's hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a ginormous Pavlova, can Alice manage to walk away with the honours?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy hopes to win with a menu of restaurant-inspired fare, but Alice and Danielle are more impressed with his Ferrari

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving student Danielle hosts the third dinner party in Leeds, hoping a high-calorie menu will see her emerge victorious. She even serves dessert wearing her wedding dress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theatre technician Matt is the fourth to host in Leeds with a menu of family favourites - and if that does not win them over, hopefully his juggling skills will do the trick

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final day of the competition in Leeds, and jeweller Lynne tries to rescue her dinner party with a memorable karaoke performance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Tower View Hotel, in Blackpool, where owner Jennifer McGirr is joined by friend Diana Carnegie. Daniel Walker and George Gillespie find some unwanted guests in the bathroom and trouble arises at breakfast when some of the guests try ordering off-menu

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit Church Spa in East Markham in Nottinghamshire on their second evening, where proprietor Daniel Walker and family friend George Gillespie hope to wow their guests with their relaxing facilities. Steve and Lisa Tindall seem impressed, but Jennifer and Diana Carnegie question the high price tag

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third hosts of the week are Steve and Lisa Tindall, who own the King's Head Inn in Rye, East Sussex, where they hope their guests will fall for their boutique B&B. They get into the swing of things with a round of blindfolded mini-golf, but not everyone gets a solid night's sleep afterwards

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former breakfast TV presenter Matt Arnold welcomes the contestants to The Bridge Hotel Brandon in Suffolk, and hopes that his mantra of 'beautiful beds, blissful bathrooms and brilliant breakfasts' shines through. The group takes part in a rowing competition in the afternoon, but delays at breakfast and a runny egg cause irritation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they have been paid and confront their critics, and things get heated when it's time to count up the cash

programme.title: Scotland's Scenic Railways
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the railways crossing the Scottish Highlands, and meeting both the workers who keep them running and the steam enthusiasts who safeguard these iconic routes. The programme takes the Strathspey steam railway through the Cairngorms National Park, taking in a charity abseiling event at the Forth Bridge along the way

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A 93-year-old war veteran may have a life-threatening infection, and a four-year-old who is pale and vomiting has to be urgently seen by a specialist

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors are concerned that a 95-year-old woman may have suffered life-changing injuries to her hip and internal bleeding after falling down a flight of stairs, while the patient's daughter reflects on her mother's optimistic attitude to life's challenges. Elsewhere, a man knocked off his bike at 40mph may have suffered brain damage. His brother remembers a difficult time that brought them closer together

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A nine-year-old boy falls over and lands on his head, and is left unable to move his limbs. As he waits for the results, the boy's father shares his feelings on the challenges of parenthood. Elsewhere, an 80-year-old woman is brought in with a dangerously swollen tongue and a nine-year-old girl is treated after falling off a swing, leaving consultant Rahail considering how the balance of care between parents and children shifts over time

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. programme.title: Live at the Apollo

programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a coach tour guide, escorting a busload of pensioners to Antwerp and Bruges. His talent with an audience proves of little use when he has to reel off a list of facts about Belgium, and he finds even getting up early every morning is major challenge

programme.title: The Indestructibles
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Adam Gendle travel around the country to complete a variety of outlandish stunts, beginning with an attempt to bounce a man across a lake and into a target

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Hampton Court Palace, which started out life as a Tudor palace in 1515 and is famous for being the home of King Henry VIII

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Schönbrunn Palace in Austria, which is located in a 500-acre park and includes an orangery, palm house and one of Europe's oldest zoos

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Special access to Salem's most mysterious sites reveals the secret history of the mass hysteria and lurid trials that led to the executions of 20 innocent people

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How in devising the F35 Lightnin II, engineers have pushed the boundaries of aviation technology further than ever before and created a brand new, revolutionary, fighter aircraft capable of handling threats in the air and on the ground. Plus, Rob Collings flies one of the most successful aircraft of the Second World War, the distinctive P38, former RAF test pilot Andy Edgell tracks down the product of a top secret research project, the X-14 and retired US Air Force pilot Mace Carpenter reconnects with the stealthy F117

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey up the west coast of Scotland and discovers how trains helped spread the reputation of Oban whisky. He also learns about the efforts to build a railway through the desolate Rannoch Moor and visits Corrour, a favourite shooting estate of the political elite in Victorian times

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Ayr to Skye and investigates the parallel 'roads' etched in the side of Glen Roy - one of the geological mysteries of the 19th century. He also finds out how the Victorians put a weather observatory on the top of Ben Nevis, and crosses the Glenfinnan viaduct by steam train

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: Since their rise in the 19th century, railways have been at the forefront of modern engineering and transport, conquering dizzying altitudes, wilderness, seas and cities

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Undercover cameras help to explore the world of animal intelligence, ingenuity and creativity in their hunting, protection and health. Spy Orang-utan meets her real-life counterparts in Borneo to capture how, living close to local people, they have gained unexpected skills in sawing wood and washing with soap, while Spy Termite catches one of the world's cleverest animal tricksters, the drongo, as he tries to outwit a group of meerkats in the battle for food

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years

programme.title: War Above the Trenches
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary about the Royal Flying Corps, recreating the aerial struggle of the First World War based on the personal accounts of pilots

programme.title: VE Day: Countdown to Peace
programme.desc: This compelling documentary celebrates the approaching 75th Anniversary of freedom and peace in Europe

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the historic Buckfast Abbey in Devon, where experts examine an 18th-century tankard, an iron chest with a hidden lock dating back to the 1640s, London street signs, a Biba frock, and a piece of shrapnel from the battleship Bismarck

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the final leg of his journey from Ayr to Skye, Michael Portillo discovers how the railways provided Second World War commandos with the means to travel to the isolated landscape of Lochailort, where they were able to train without attracting the attention of German spies. He also learns why langoustines have replaced herring as the best catch in the fishing port of Mallaig, and explores the history of Highland crofters

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns more about the country's railways, beginning with the Great Eastern Line, which runs along the edge of England and into central London. On his travels, the former politician discovers the grave-robbing history of Great Yarmouth and tries his hand at working a Victorian swing bridge in Reedham, Norfolk. He also takes to the air to reveal how a rail guidebook helped aviators during the Second World War

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Hidden in a remote mountainous forest is a structure nicknamed the Fly Trap or Hitler's Stonehenge. Researchers still have no definitive idea what it is

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The dark tale of the skeleton of an unknown woman found in a hollowed-out tree in Birmingham woodland, exploring the links between the woman and Nazi spy rings operating in Britain

programme.title: WW2 - Frontlines
programme.desc: On January 22nd 1944, two divisions land at the Italian port of Anzio in one of the easiest Allied amphibious assaults of the war

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: A look at how British-trained Czechs were behind the assassination of Reinhard Heydrich, one of the main architects of the Holocaust

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: How Second World War criminal Adolf Eichmann was tracked down to Buenos Aires, Argentina, in 1961

programme.desc: The Royal Palace of Caserta near Naples, Italy, which was created by Luigi Vanvitelli and houses a silk factory that dates back to the 18th century and still functions to this day

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Kensington Palace in London, which began life as an early 17th-century Jacobean mansion before becoming a palace fit for the monarchy, and was the brainchild of Christopher Wren

programme.desc: Special access to cutting-edge archaeological investigations in the Holy Land unearth fresh evidence that reveals new secrets about the life of Moses

programme.desc: A look at the incredible machines built to get troops directly to the front line, on land, at sea and in the air

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The Independent, a skyscraper in Austin, Texas. At a height of 690ft, it is the tallest building in the city, surpassing the 683ft Austonian, another residential skyscraper

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the final leg of his journey from Ayr to Skye, Michael Portillo discovers how the railways provided Second World War commandos with the means to travel to the isolated landscape of Lochailort, where they were able to train without attracting the attention of German spies. He also learns why langoustines have replaced herring as the best catch in the fishing port of Mallaig, and explores the history of Highland crofters

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns more about the country's railways, beginning with the Great Eastern Line, which runs along the edge of England and into central London. On his travels, the former politician discovers the grave-robbing history of Great Yarmouth and tries his hand at working a Victorian swing bridge in Reedham, Norfolk. He also takes to the air to reveal how a rail guidebook helped aviators during the Second World War

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha. Other finds being valued include an intricate model of a vintage car made by a prisoner of war, an Elizabethan ring, a collection of classic guitars, and of course, a great variety of Concorde memorabilia

programme.title: Impossible Railways
programme.desc: How some of our greatest railways have challenged engineering's finest minds in their efforts to conquer the planet's waterways, including a tunnel beneath the English Channel.

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: The undercover cameras reveal how animals rely upon each other for a range of activities. Arctic wolves group together to survive in one of the harshest landscapes on earth, a band of hungry mongoose groom warthogs for tasty morsels - before turning their attention to Spy Warthog - and fish help clean hippos' bodies, as well as providing a unique dental service. Meanwhile, crocodiles and dikkop birds take part in a mutual neighbourhood watch against hungry prowling monitor lizards, and Spy Rattlesnake captures how prairie dogs and burrowing owls use their language skills to tell the rest of their community of approaching danger

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A ruined compound on the edge of a peninsula in Colombia which was once home to a famous outlaw and the strange towers looming over red-tinted waters in a Portuguese landscape

programme.title: Wartime Crime
programme.desc: Documentary about criminals in Britain during the Second World War, beginning with how London gangster William Charles Hill made his mark on the Black Market

programme.title: Churchill's Bodyguard
programme.desc: Insight into the work of Inspector Walter Thompson, the man assigned to protect Winston Churchill from the early 1920s until the end of the Second World War. The detective saves the politician's life in an attack by the fledgling IRA, during a time of anarchism, spies and suffragettes

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Concorde Hangar at the Aerospace Bristol museum, where the most surprising item brought in is a very valuable statue of Buddha. Other finds being valued include an intricate model of a vintage car made by a prisoner of war, an Elizabethan ring, a collection of classic guitars, and of course, a great variety of Concorde memorabilia

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey along the Great Eastern Line, which ventures from the edge of England to the heart of London. In this leg, he examines the Victorians' fascination with the Suffolk village of Dunwich, Britain's answer to Atlantis, and discovers how nearby Felixstowe became home to the UK's biggest container port

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits the sites of Victorian freak shows, rides one of the world's first electric railways in Southend and encounters the preserved head of a medieval politician as he continues his journey along the route of the Great Eastern Line

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: At the East Lancashire Railway, the annual Race the Train event gets under way - with athletes of all abilities taking on the 11-mile course from Bury to Rawtenstall

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor

programme.desc: Derek heads to Suffolk to collect two Bentleys with a majestic pedigree; Cynthia a stunning 1963 S3 and Harry, a 1965 Continental

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave heads to West Yorkshire to collect a rare 1926 Dodge bus. Derek gets his hands on a rare Vauxhall Victor VX 90 police car that is expected to sell for more than a few coppers

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: The Royal Palace of Caserta near Naples, Italy, which was created by Luigi Vanvitelli and houses a silk factory that dates back to the 18th century and still functions to this day

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of Neuschwanstein Castle in the Alps of southern Germany. Created for eccentric king Ludwig the second in the 19th century, the castle is both a visual and engineering marvel that was an influence on Walt Disney. Containing a huge throne room and man-made cave, it took 17 years to build but was never fully completed after Ludwig was deposed, incarcerated and mysteriously died in 1868

programme.title: World's Greatest Palaces

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: The remarkable story of how Stalin's massive war factories were dismantled and moved east on one and half million railway trucks, to avoid being lost to the Nazis

programme.title: War Factories

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling a year in the existence of 1,000-year-old Windsor Castle and its inhabitants, from the housekeeper and chefs to the Queen herself, who has allowed her public and private life to be documented more extensively than ever before. The state visit of former President of France Jacques Chirac sees the staff organise a banquet and a performance of a West End musical inside the building - but protesters outside threaten to mar proceedings

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Knights of the Garter arrive at Windsor Castle for their annual lunch with members of the monarchy, which includes a complicated procession through the palace. The royal archives allow cameras to view nearly 200 years of photo albums, including the thousands of photographs from the Queen Mother's collection, and the castle clock-maker faces a race to ensure all 450 timepieces on the estate are turned back

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Duke of Edinburgh, in his capacity as Ranger of the Great Park, takes a tour of Windsor's grounds and points out the modifications he has made during his tenure, including a deer park and a farm shop selling local produce. programme.desc: Victor and Margaret invite Mrs Warboys to stay at their house until her flooded flat dries out. However, they soon come to regret their generous offer as she begins taking over the entire household. Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Doreen Mantle star

programme.desc: Having inherited some money, Hampshire couple Phil and Bernie Smith are now in a position of being able to afford a holiday home. They have decided that south Tenerife's warm winter climate is ideal for whenever Phil's work as a gardener in the UK slows down, so Scarlette Douglas shows them five apartments within their £100,000 budget for them to choose between

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse enthusiast Richard Negus hosts the first dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, focusing on a 'day at the races' theme. He serves a course called Under Starter's Orders, and They're Off-al for his main course, and provides a game of snail racing for entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Business studies lecturer Amber Elba Porter hosts the second dinner party in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, hoping her spiritual-themed evening, complete with tarot card readings and rune stones, will win her the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tim Reinman presents the third dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, but is upstaged by domestic goddess Hannah Totman, leaving spiritualist Amber Elba-Porter and hypnotherapist Sue Smith less than happy with their fellow guest. The host hopes his 'Timoffee' pie will help to get the night back on track

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue Smith hosts the fourth dinner party from Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, and decides to organise an evening of entertainment revolving around a 'back to school' theme. However, the items on her menu puzzle the guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Self-styled domestic goddess Hannah Totman hosts the final dinner party in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, and hopes that her Italian-themed menu and opera singing will impress her guests enough for her to win the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

Action adventure sequel, starring Tom Cruise, Jeremy Renner, Simon Pegg and Paula Patton

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Bromsgrove treat a 52-year-old man suffering sudden, severe headaches, while Manni Suman and Lucy McGraynor look after an unconscious patient. In Worcester, paramedics Ed Hird and Nick Carson have to negotiate a garden fence to reach a 65-year-old biker with a shattered leg

programme.title: Big Dog Britain
programme.desc: With sales of the very biggest breeds in decline, this documentary meets the owners who are overshadowed by their pets in a bid to discover why larger canines are being overlooked. programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Gary and Susie Gillhooly, who want to start a new adventure in Florida's beautiful Sarasota Keys. Jonnie shows them how far their budget will stretch in this highly desirable corner of the US

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Data analyst John Tower hosts the second dinner party in north Kent, and lets his passion for military re-enactment show when he barks orders at the table. However, not all the guests enjoy his fatty pork stew and psychedelic dessert, and two argue over dating etiquette

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Project manager Yemi Christopher-Soares hosts the third dinner party in north Kent, where she hopes the guests will enjoy her Nigerian-inspired menu and appreciate her love of all things spicy

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jono Simm hosts the penultimate dinner party in north Kent and is convinced his wholesome and hearty menu is the way to win. However, two of his guests are less than impressed by his cooking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Party-loving Lisa Townend hosts the final dinner party in north Kent, and hopes that a menu full of French fancies and innovative entertainment in the form of human roulette, will be enough to impress her guests and see her crowned winner of the £1,000 cash prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.desc: The architect meets a father and son building an ambitious clifftop retreat and a young architect embarking on his first commission - a garden pavilion for his grandmother. programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George meets a father-of-three building a campervan for less than £700, made from old CDs and vinyl records, and a single dad who is renovating an oil-rig escape pod to make a home for himself and sons on the water. The presenter also continues his German road trip with a visit to the Tyrolean mountains, where he finds a house that looks like a cross between a large bird and a UFO, and he and Will Hardie save a decrepit old wartime lookout shed to use as the basis for their floating beach hut

They are supported by probation and police, doing the best they can, but large caseloads and a lack of resources leave officers overwhelmed - and when it's the job of probation to manage and assess the risk posed by offenders in the community, the consequences of mistakes can be disastrous

programme.title: Naked Attraction

programme.title: The Super Orgasm
programme.desc: For most women multiple orgasms can seem almost mythical. But some can experience it not once, twice or even 10 times, but 60, 70 or 100 - a super-orgasm. In this documentary, a group of these women volunteer for a series of tests in a scientific investigation to reveal the truth behind the super-orgasm. The aim is to measure and record the phenomenon for the first time, finding out how the brain and the body react before, during and after, and asking if these women are wired differently to other people. And if not, is it something that could be learned by anyone?

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals

Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles

programme.title: We Are Lady Parts
programme.desc: Publicly shamed, shunned by her university friends and out of Lady Parts, Amina is at an all-time low, so is grateful when Noor offers to help her get back on track - firstly by finding a respectable man to marry who will overlook her transgressions. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Wendy and Marcus give their wedding barn a last-minute makeover as they prepare to host their first official booking: a steampunk-themed wedding vow renewal. Meanwhile, near the Loire Valley, chateau owners Ben and Billie make the most of their riverside location by building a floating pontoon as part of their plans for a bijou glamping site

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sun-worshippers Carol and Junior Smith have cashed in their savings and cannot wait to buy a holiday home where they can spend more quality time as a family. Will they go for a bungalow with a rooftop terrace or a spacious townhouse that has them lost for words?

programme.desc: John and Poppy are hoping to retire to France's Lot-et-Garonne region, so Ben Hillman sets out to find them the perfect place for their £175,000 budget

programme.desc: Lisa resolves to pursue a career in astronomy, but unfortunately her decision coincides with Chief Wiggum's hare-brained new initiative to reduce crime in Springfield - by illuminating the whole town all night. With the guest voice of Eric Idle

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer falls out with Marge over revelations contained in an old letter, and moves into the home of a gay couple who keep him happy by plying him with food. With the guest voice of 'Weird Al' Yankovic

programme.desc: The family spends Christmas on a ranch, where Lisa falls in love with an older boy, and Homer and Bart help locals protesting against a dam built by industrious beavers. Featuring the guest voices of David Byrne, Jonathan Taylor Thomas and Andy Serkis

programme.desc: Expats Russell and Lindsay hope to attract punters to their convenience store with a selection of pastries, while retired nurse Susie and her husband Reg, who volunteer at a dog shelter, have their hands full with a littler of 10 puppies. Life coach Svetlana's online rants about the behaviour of expats on the Costa del Sol have gone viral. But as she prepares to record another monologue, she has a surprise up her sleeve

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Paul Walker and Victoria Maude leave their five-bedroom house in Arun Valley, West Sussex behind to trade places with Steve and Naz Edney, who live in a three-bedroom house in a Fareham, Hampshire, and are in the country's poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: A decade of health problems has resulted in Elaine's two-bedroom flat in Rochester, Kent, being in dire need of a deep clean, but cat sick on the floor and an infestation of fleas make it one of the toughest jobs that Jon and Brennan have ever taken on

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A high-speed chase in Bradford's crowded streets ends very suddenly and the driver claims he just wants to get home to eat his takeaway while it is still hot. But is that the whole story? In Harrogate, a volatile drink-driver's rapid mood swings prove a test for the cops, while a large stash of cannabis is found at the scene of a major collision, which has left one person with life-threatening injuries. A couple suspected of dealing drugs are chased down and taken to the station, where they provide the traffic team with contrasting reactions to being held in custody. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Thieves dragging a stolen generator on one wheel along town and country roads cause chaos, smashing the barriers on a closed level crossing and coming close to causing a train crash. A van with a surprising load is tracked using state-of-the-art technology in a game of cat and mouse, and a high-stakes hunt for a stolen van and mini-digger on the M1 nearly leads to carnage. Jamie Theakston presents

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Carlin arrests a gang of van thieves and uncovers a major criminal operation in the process, while a late-night compost thief is caught on his way out of the shower. Officers hunt for a trucker wielding a baseball bat who has apparently succumbed to road rage and vegetarian Craggy rides to the rescue of a lorry load of calves on the A46. Elsewhere, Brian cracks down on a barbaric blood sport, Jon faces an unexploded bomb, and two fuel thieves plough straight into the interceptors' precious BMW

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit are at the forefront of a raid against suspected drug traffickers, the hunt is on for a driver who has fled the scene of a horrific crash, and Rich Precious and Sean Baxter are left lagging far behind as they chase a speeding biker. Dan Brigginshaw is on duty at Spalding's annual flower show, and Sean Brennan deals with a young man who thinks he can throw his weight around after a boozy night out

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A van thief gives officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit the runaround before taking a disastrous wrong turn. Dan joins a manhunt but finds dodgy diesel instead, while Jon wades into a late-night assault involving a pair of TV legends. Big Jim reports a boy racer to his mum and delivers a dressing down to a driver with a taste for rude gestures

programme.desc: Pierce is annoyed to learn Nicolette planted the necklace to ensure Chloe learned of his affair. Amy asks Toadie if she can move into Number 30

programme.desc: Ziggy's job search gets her down. Nikau is pressured to take appetite suppressants after a day-in-the-life photo shoot meant to increase his social media presence

Cameras also follow former Playboy bunny Michelle and her husband Tony, who have taken advantage of the Costa's roasting heat and bought an awning company

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: The hospital struggles with a huge backlog of untreated patients from the night shift, as staff have a hard time ensuring that everyone is discharged or admitted within four hours of arrival. An 85-year-old motorist is treated for a severe head injury after skidding on ice, her car ending up on its roof. A woman who tripped while shopping fears that a broken ankle will jeopardise her plans to go on a cruise

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: The specialist clinic opens its doors to patients with acne, psoriasis and cysts.

programme.title: 999: Critical Condition
programme.desc: Thirty-eight-year-old Lesley is rushed in when her heart stops while she is driving to work, leaving Dr Morris battle to resuscitate her and investigate why a normally fit and well woman with no known medical problems would suffer such an attack.
